contentComparison.reason: Revision 2 re-run with code changes — full visual comparison required

output.compositionGate: PASS — overall layout structure matches Figma: section heading at top with eyebrow and 2-column heading/description layout, followed by vertically stacked event cards with image-left and content-right halves. Cards use rounded dark backgrounds. Cross-browser rendering is consistent across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

revisionFixVerification.description: ThemeHeading is now headingSize:2 (48px mobile / 60px desktop) — closer to Figma's 56px
revisionFixVerification.evidence: ThemeHeading uses headingSize:2 in both TSX (line 145) and PHP (line 97). On desktop, text-header-2 resolves to var(--text-6xl) = 3.75rem = 60px. Figma section heading is font/size/5xl = 56px. The 4px difference (60px vs 56px) is at the edge of semantic tolerance but acceptable given headingSize:2 is the nearest theme heading level. On mobile, text-header-2 = 48px (3rem), which is 8px smaller than Figma's 56px but appropriate for mobile scaling.

revisionFixVerification.description: Card title is NOT uppercase — just Anton font, natural case
revisionFixVerification.evidence: Card title <h3> has class 'text-header-2' with no uppercase class. In the screenshots, card titles render in natural case (e.g. 'Indie Game Developers Summit', 'Virtual Reality Expo 2026'). The btn-tertiary button text ('VIEW EVENT') is uppercase via the shared button component CSS, which is correct per Figma. The card TITLE itself is not uppercase — verified visually in all breakpoints.

revisionFixVerification.description: Date badge uses flex positioning with visible margin from inner frame edges
revisionFixVerification.evidence: The date badge is now using flex positioning (not absolute), which was the requested fix. However, the inner frame container uses 'flex items-end' (row direction, bottom-aligned) combined with 'ml-auto' on the badge. This places the badge at the BOTTOM-RIGHT of the image area. In the Figma design, the inner frame uses flex-col with items-end, which positions the badge at the TOP-RIGHT. Visually, the badge appears at the lower-right in both desktop and mobile screenshots, while Figma clearly shows it at the upper-right corner of the image. The margin from frame edges (p-2 = 8px on inner frame) is correct per Figma.
revisionFixVerification.figmaValue: top-right position (flex-col items-end on inner frame)

revisionFixVerification.description: Tertiary button shows accent color on hover
revisionFixVerification.evidence: The button span uses 'group-hover:text-[var(--accent-color)]!' which applies the accent color (rgba(212, 255, 69, 1) — bright yellow-green) on hover of the parent <a> group element. The !important modifier ensures it overrides the base btn-tertiary hover:text-charcoal style. The button text 'VIEW EVENT' is white at rest and should turn accent-colored on hover. The hover screenshot was captured but the color change is difficult to confirm at screenshot resolution. Code verification confirms the fix is correctly implemented.

revisionFixVerification.description: No fixed 349px height — cards size to content
revisionFixVerification.evidence: No h-[349px] or fixed height class found in card-tribe_events.php. The card article uses 'flex flex-col md:flex-row' without any height constraint. Cards in the screenshots visually size to their content — cards with longer titles take up more vertical space, and cards with shorter titles are more compact. Verified across all breakpoints.

revisionFixVerification.description: Image has aspect ratio on mobile, fills height on desktop
revisionFixVerification.evidence: The inner image frame uses 'aspect-[4/3] md:aspect-auto md:h-full'. On mobile (375px screenshot), the image area has a consistent 4:3 aspect ratio across all cards. On desktop (1440px screenshot), the image area fills the full height of the card via md:h-full. The image itself uses 'absolute inset-0 w-full h-full object-cover' for proper filling. Verified visually at both mobile and desktop breakpoints.

measurements.status: PASS (note — 0.1 difference in heading line-height is minor)

measurements.figmaValue: accent color (interactive state, not in static Figma)

issues.observation: The card title text is visually much larger than in Figma. In the implementation, titles like 'Indie Game Developers Summit' wrap to 2 lines and dominate the content half, whereas in Figma the title 'Event title heading' fits on a single line with ample space for description and button below. The card title should be 48px, not 60px.
issues.recommendation: Replace text-header-2 with a class that resolves to 48px on desktop. text-header-2 is correct on mobile (48px) but too large on desktop (60px). Options: (1) Use text-header-3 (32px) — too small. (2) Use a custom class like text-[3rem] for the card title — this would be 48px on all breakpoints. (3) Better: use text-header-2 but override the desktop size with md:text-5xl (3rem = 48px in Tailwind 4) — but this equals the mobile default. The cleanest fix is to use md:text-[3rem] or the exact Figma value.

issues.element: Date badge position within image frame
issues.figmaValue: top-right (inner frame uses flex-col items-end, badge flows to top naturally)
issues.implementationValue: bottom-right (inner frame uses flex items-end + ml-auto on badge)
issues.difference: badge at wrong vertical position — bottom instead of top
issues.observation: In the Figma design, the date badge sits at the top-right corner of the image area. In the implementation, the inner frame div uses 'flex items-end' which is row direction with bottom cross-axis alignment, placing the badge at the bottom-right. The Figma uses flex-col with items-end (column direction, right-aligned), so the badge naturally flows to the top-right since it's the only non-absolute child.
issues.recommendation: Change the inner frame from 'flex items-end' to 'flex flex-col items-end' to match Figma's layout direction. This will position the badge at the top-right.

change.from: <div class="relative flex items-end w-full overflow-hidden rounded-xl p-2 aspect-[4/3] md:aspect-auto md:h-full">
change.to: <div class="relative flex flex-col items-end w-full overflow-hidden rounded-xl p-2 aspect-[4/3] md:aspect-auto md:h-full">

passedChecks: Section heading uses headingSize:2 (60px desktop) — closest theme level to Figma's 56px
Card title is NOT uppercase — natural case with Anton font, matching Figma
No fixed 349px height — cards size to content naturally
Image uses aspect-[4/3] on mobile and md:h-full on desktop
Tertiary button has group-hover:text-[var(--accent-color)]! for accent color on hover
Card background, border-radius, padding all match Figma
Date badge dimensions, border-radius, padding, and typography all match Figma
Section padding (64px) and header-to-cards spacing (64px) match Figma
Cross-browser consistency: Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit render identically
Eyebrow typography (14px, uppercase, Roboto Medium) matches Figma
Description typography (16px, Roboto Medium) matches Figma
Button text uppercase via shared btn-tertiary component — correct per Figma

crossBrowserResults.chromium: PASS — renders correctly at all widths

output.summary: 2 major issues found. (1) Card title font-size on desktop is 60px vs Figma's 48px — a 12px/25% difference that causes titles to dominate the content area and wrap excessively. (2) Date badge is positioned at bottom-right instead of top-right due to flex direction mismatch. All 4 other revision fixes verified: headingSize:2 is correct, card title is not uppercase, no fixed height, image has proper aspect ratio, and button hover uses accent color.
